title: Digital Compass: Seeweb stringe una partnership con VSIX per migliorare i servizi cloud nel Nord-Est
subtitle: Mercoledì 15 maggio a Padova, l’evento “Insieme per potenziare la connettività Internet del Nord-Est” per spiegare i risultati raggiunti e le prossime sfide
content: Migliorare la connettività e i servizi cloud nelle Regioni del Nord-Est, per raggiungere i traguardi fissati dal Digital Compass 2030 adottato dalla Commissione Europea. È l’obiettivo della partnership che Il Digital Compass conferma che la transizione digitale ha un ruolo primario per lo sviluppo dell’economia comunitaria, e prescrive che entro il 2030 il 90% delle imprese europee raggiungano un livello base di intensità digitale. Un target che l’Italia sta cercando di conquistare anche grazie agli investimenti stanziati con il Pnrr per creare reti ultraveloci e digitalizzare la PA. Le imprese italiane sono in linea con la media europea per quanto attiene l’adozione tecnologica: la maggior parte delle PMI italiane, infatti, ha almeno un livello base di intensità digitale. Evidenziano però fortissime differenze a seconda della tecnologie adottate. In particolare, infatti, le aziende italiane hanno investito molto più delle colleghe europee nel cloud computing, mentre sono fortemente al di sotto della media nell’utilizzo di tecnologie avanzate come l’AI o i big data. “Il Veneto è una delle regioni italiane più produttive, insieme a Lombardia e Veneto - sottolinea Antonio Baldassarra, Ceo di Seeweb. - “Infatti, figura tra le prime 10 in Europa per livello di valore aggiunto industriale. In ragione di questo siamo molto contenti di una partnership che ci consente di stare al passo con tali ritmi di crescita attraverso il digitale e, oggi, attraverso nuove tecnologie come per esempio quelle di GPU Computing per IA e machine learning, che possono efficientare e velocizzare ancora di più la produttività. Grazie agli investimenti effettuati, possiamo dire che Seeweb è oggi l’unico punto di riferimento sul fronte italiano”. “VSIX è nata nel 2009 per migliorare la qualità della connettività Internet nel Nord-Est – spiega Eleonora Di Maria, Presidente di VSIX. - Consente agli operatori e ai principali enti del territorio di scambiare traffico e servizi in un nodo neutrale, ad alta efficienza, scalabilità e sicurezza, permettendo di ottimizzare le risorse di comunicazione a lunga distanza. Grazie alla qualità dei servizi offerti, VSIX ha un traffico medio su base annuale di circa 50Gbps, ed è il quarto nodo di interscambio italiano per volume di traffico pubblico e privato raggiunto, dopo Milano, Roma e Torino. La collaborazione con Seeweb consente a VSIX di poter ampliare la propria capacità di stimolo e accompagnamento del tessuto imprenditoriale del Nord-est verso una digitalizzazione evoluta – conclude Di Maria, - mettendo a fattor comune le reciproche specializzazioni e il potenziale di ricerca e innovazione dell’Ateneo di Padova”.
en_title: Digital Compass: Seeweb is partnering with VSIX to improve cloud services in North East
en_content: Improve connectivity and cloud services in the North-East Regions to reach the goals set by the Digital Compass 2030 adopted by the European Commission. It is the aim of the partnership that Digital Compass confirms that the digital transition plays a primary role in the development of the Community economy, and prescribes that by 2030 90% of European companies reach a basic level of digital intensity. A target that Italy is trying to conquer also thanks to investments allocated with the Pnrr to create ultra-fast networks and digitize the PA. Italian companies are in line with the European average for technological adoption: most Italian SMEs, in fact, have at least one basic level of digital intensity. However, they highlight very strong differences depending on the technologies adopted. In particular, in fact, Italian companies have invested much more than European colleagues in cloud computing, while they are strongly below average in the use of advanced technologies such as AI or big data. "The Veneto is one of the most productive Italian regions, together with Lombardy and Veneto - underlines Antonio Baldassarra, Ceo di Seeweb. - “In fact, it is among the top 10 in Europe for added value in industry. Because of this we are very happy with a partnership that allows us to keep up with such growth rates through digital and, today, through new technologies such as GPU Computing for IA and machine learning, which can improve and speed even more productivity. Thanks to the investments made, we can say that Seeweb is today the only reference point on the Italian front”. “VSIX was born in 2009 to improve the quality of Internet connectivity in the North East – explains Eleonora Di Maria, President of VSIX. - Enables operators and main local authorities to exchange traffic and services in a neutral, high-efficiency, scalability and security node, allowing to optimize long-distance communication resources. Thanks to the quality of the services offered, VSIX has an average traffic on an annual basis of about 50Gbps, and is the fourth knot of Italian interchange for volume of public and private traffic reached, after Milan, Rome and Turin. The collaboration with Seeweb allows VSIX to be able to expand its ability to stimulate and accompani the entrepreneurial fabric of the North-East towards an advanced digitization – concludes Di Maria, - putting to common the reciprocal specializations and the potential of research and innovation of the University of Padua".
en_subtitle: Wednesday, May 15 in Padua, the event “Together to strengthen the Internet connectivity of the Northeast” to explain the results achieved and the next challenges

title: DeepL lancia DeepL Write Pro per aiutare le aziende a ottimizzare la comunicazione
content: Colonia, Germania – DeepL, leader globale dell’IA linguistica, presenta DeepL Write Pro, il primo servizio di DeepL a essere alimentato dai suoi modelli linguistici di grandi dimensioni (LLM). DeepL Write Pro rivoluziona la stesura di qualsiasi testo, sia per la comunicazione interna sia esterna, aiutando così le aziende a interfacciarsi con il mercato globale in modo accurato e sicuro. DeepL Write Pro, al contrario degli strumenti di IA generativa che propongono testi già scritti e dei tradizionali correttori ortografici e grammaticali, funge da assistente creativo durante il processo di scrittura. Offre infatti suggerimenti in tempo reale sulla scelta delle parole e su come rendere le frasi più scorrevoli, oltre a permettere di riscrivere i testi secondo un dato stile o tono. Questo approccio unico nel suo genere è alla base di una sinergia creativa tra l’utente e l’IA in grado di trasformare i testi senza sacrificare la voce autentica di chi scrive. Tramite DeepL Write Pro è possibile rifinire i testi in inglese e tedesco anche se non si hanno competenze da madrelingua, dando a chiunque gli strumenti per esprimersi al meglio in qualsiasi contesto. “Il nostro team di Corporate Communications utilizza volentieri DeepL Write, in particolare per rifinire i materiali scritti in tedesco e in inglese” sostiene un’azienda di retail che ricorre a DeepL Write, sottolineandone il ruolo fondamentale nell’ottimizzazione della comunicazione nel team. Tra i punti forti di DeepL Write Pro spiccano le funzioni mirate alla massima sicurezza. Secondo lo studio Data Privacy Benchmark di Cisco del 2024, la maggior parte delle organizzazioni stanno limitando ai dipendenti l’utilizzo dell’IA generativa per timori sulla tutela dei dati personali. Il 98% degli intervistati, inoltre, dichiara che le certificazioni esterne sulla privacy sono un fattore importante nella decisione di acquisto.* L’eliminazione immediata dei testi e la crittografia TLS, due delle funzioni di sicurezza incluse nell’ultimo prodotto di DeepL, sono quindi dei vantaggi da non sottovalutare per le aziende. DeepL Write Pro prende le mosse dalla rinomata tecnologia di un’azienda il cui prodotto di punta, DeepL Traduttore, viene utilizzato da oltre 100 000 aziende in tutto il mondo per la sua precisione e sicurezza. Per i clienti attuali di DeepL Traduttore, l’aggiunta di DeepL Write Pro crea una suite di strumenti di comunicazione ideali per le aziende che vogliono sfruttare un’IA linguistica all’avanguardia per favorire la crescita internazionale. “Le parole contano e la lingua può rappresentare il fattore determinante per avere successo all’estero” afferma Jarek Kutylowski, fondatore e amministratore delegato di DeepL. “DeepL Write Pro è il primo prodotto di DeepL a essere alimentato dai nostri LLM, e rappresenta il culmine di anni e anni di ricerca e innovazione che ci hanno distinto rispetto agli altri giganti del tech. I servizi offerti da DeepL e la sua IA linguistica forniscono una soluzione completa per le aziende che desiderano trasformare e migliorare il modo in cui comunicano, stimolando al tempo stesso la creatività”. Secondo alcuni studi specialistici, una scarsa qualità dei testi costa alle aziende miliardi di dollari all’anno. Un recente sondaggio di Forbes condotto su 1000 impiegati statunitensi, infatti, evidenzia come una comunicazione poco curata abbia avuto un impatto negativo sulla produttività per il 49% degli intervistati, sulla soddisfazione professionale per il 50% e sui livelli di stress per il 42%.** DeepL Write Pro è disponibile in inglese e tedesco, con altre lingue in arrivo. Grazie ai modelli linguistici di grandi dimensioni (LLM) è in grado di riscrivere i testi in base allo stile (accademico, aziendale, colloquiale o semplice) o al tono (amichevole, deciso, diplomatico o entusiasta) selezionato dall’utente. Non si limita quindi a migliorare il modo di scrivere, ma stimola anche la creatività. “Trovo DeepL Write molto utile. Alcuni miei colleghi lo utilizzano regolarmente non solo per scrivere meglio, ma anche per farsi venire idee su come riformulare gli stessi concetti in altri modi” sostiene un avvocato di uno studio legale internazionale. Con un abbonamento a DeepL Write Pro è possibile correggere e migliorare testi senza limiti con la massima protezione dei dati, garantendo così una comunicazione sempre efficace e sicura. “Utilizzare DeepL Write è come avere un revisore personale che si assicura che i report e i materiali di marketing siano sempre perfetti” dichiara una società di consulenza a livello mondiale. “È particolarmente utile per chi non è madrelingua inglese. Il modo in cui siamo riusciti a perfezionare i nostri documenti è qualcosa di straordinario. E non si tratta solo di correggere gli errori, ma anche di migliorare in inglese e di comunicare in maniera più efficace con i clienti”. DeepL fornisce soluzioni di IA linguistica di alta qualità per organizzazioni internazionali di qualsiasi dimensione e settore, dalle piccole imprese alle multinazionali, fino agli enti governativi. È grazie alla combinazione di tre elementi che supera i giganti del tech: la ricerca impareggiabile nell’ambito dell’IA, l’addestramento di modelli basati su dati di alta qualità raccolti tramite web crawler specializzati e, non da ultimo, il contributo di linguisti esperti che assicurano l’accuratezza dei prodotti. L’intero stack tecnologico di DeepL è di sua proprietà, ulteriore garanzia della massima sicurezza dei dati per le aziende che vogliono prosperare a livello globale. Per scoprire come acquistare DeepL Write Pro per te o il tuo team, Informazioni su DeepL DeepL è leader a livello mondiale dell’IA linguistica. Il suo prodotto di punta, DeepL Traduttore, fornisce dal 2017 traduzioni automatiche altamente accurate, abbattendo le barriere linguistiche per le aziende da ogni parte del mondo. DeepL Write, assistente di scrittura a sua volta alimentato dall’IA, è una risorsa preziosa per la scrittura di testi professionali che rivoluziona il modo di comunicare. L’IA linguistica di DeepL coniuga le competenze di ricercatori e sviluppatori con quelle di esperti linguisti, creando così degli strumenti in grado di dare alle aziende un vantaggio competitivo grazie a traduzioni precise, tecnologie innovative e alla massima sicurezza dei dati. L’azienda, guidata dal fondatore e amministratore delegato Jaroslaw (Jarek) Kutylowski, è supportata da investitori di fama mondiale, tra cui IVP, Benchmark e b2venture. ––––––––––––  Press Contact:
en_title: DeepL launches DeepL Write Pro to help companies optimize communication
en_content: Cologne, Germany – DeepL, a global leader of linguistic AI, presents DeepL Write Pro, DeepL's first service to be powered by its large language models (LLM). DeepL Write Pro revolutionizes the writing of any text, both for internal and external communication, thus helping companies to interface with the global market accurately and safely. DeepL Write Pro, contrary to the tools of IA generative that propose already written texts and traditional spelling and grammar correctors, acts as a creative assistant during the writing process. It offers real-time suggestions on the choice of words and on how to make phrases more sliding, as well as allowing to rewrite texts according to a given style or tone. This unique approach is the basis of a creative synergy between the user and AI that can transform the texts without sacrificing the authentic voice of those who write. Through DeepL Write Pro you can finish the texts in English and German even if you do not have skills as a native speaker, giving anyone the tools to best express themselves in any context. “Our Corporate Communications team gladly uses DeepL Write, in particular to finish the materials written in German and English,” says a retail company that uses DeepL Write, underlining its fundamental role in optimising communication in the team. Among the strong points of DeepL Write Pro stands out for maximum safety. According to Cisco's Data Privacy Benchmark study of 2024, most organizations are limiting the use of the AI for fears on personal data protection. 98% of respondents also declare that external privacy certifications are an important factor in the purchase decision. * The immediate elimination of texts and TLS encryption, two of the security features included in DeepL’s latest product, are therefore advantages not to be underestimated for companies. DeepL Write Pro takes its cue from the renowned technology of a company whose flagship product, DeepL Translator, is used by over 100,000 companies worldwide for its precision and safety. For current customers of DeepL Translator, adding DeepL Write Pro creates a suite of communication tools ideal for companies that want to exploit a cutting-edge language AI to promote international growth. “The words matter and the language can be the determining factor for success abroad,” says Jarek Kutylowski, founder and CEO of DeepL. “DeepL Write Pro is DeepL's first product to be powered by our LLM, and represents the culmination of years and years of research and innovation that have distinguished us from other tech giants. The services offered by DeepL and its linguistic IA provide a complete solution for companies wishing to transform and improve the way they communicate, while stimulating creativity." According to some specialist studies, poor quality of texts costs billions of dollars a year. A recent survey of Forbes conducted on 1000 US employees, in fact, shows that a poorly maintained communication had a negative impact on productivity for 49% of respondents, professional satisfaction for 50% and stress levels for 42%.**** DeepL Write Pro is available in English and German, with other languages coming. Thanks to the large language models (LLM) it is able to rewrite the texts according to the style (academic, business, colloquial or simple) or tone (friendly, decided, diplomatic or enthusiastic) selected by the user. It is therefore not limited to improving the way of writing, but also stimulates creativity. “I find DeepL Write very useful. Some of my colleagues regularly use it not only to write better, but also to get ideas on how to reformulate the same concepts in other ways,” says a lawyer from an international law firm. With a subscription to DeepL Write Pro you can correct and improve unlimited texts with maximum data protection, thus ensuring always effective and secure communication. “Using DeepL Write is like having a personal reviewer that ensures that reports and marketing materials are always perfect,” says a global consulting firm. “It is particularly useful for those who are not native English speakers. The way we managed to perfect our documents is something extraordinary. And it’s not just about correcting mistakes, but also improving in English and communicating more effectively with customers.” DeepL provides high-quality linguistic IA solutions for international organizations of any size and industry, from small businesses to multinationals, to government agencies. It is thanks to the combination of three elements that exceed the tech giants: the unparalleled research within AI, the training of high-quality data-based models collected through specialized web crawlers and, last but not least, the contribution of experienced linguists who ensure the accuracy of products. DeepL’s entire technology stack is its property, further guarantee of maximum data security for companies that want to thrive globally. To find out how to buy DeepL Write Pro for you or your team, DeepL DeepL is a world leader in linguistic AI. Its flagship product, DeepL Translator, provides highly accurate automatic translations since 2017, lowering language barriers for companies from all over the world. DeepL Write, an AI-powered writing assistant, is a valuable resource for writing professional texts that revolutionizes how to communicate. DeepL’s linguistic AI combines the skills of researchers and developers with those of linguist experts, thus creating tools that can give companies a competitive advantage thanks to precise translations, innovative technologies and maximum data security. The company, led by founder and CEO Jaroslaw (Jarek) Kutylowski, is supported by world-famous investors, including IVP, Benchmark and b2venture. ––––––––––––– Press Contact:

title: Nillion is pleased to announce that Dwinity, a team pioneering decentralized AI has joined ...
content: With huge amounts of personal data being collected every day, privacy concerns are escalating as the impact of data breaches become more costly. The need to address the problems created by the existing data economy have never been greater. By empowering users to reclaim control, Dwinity aims to unlock an enormous amount of potential for personal data to enhance various industries like healthcare, insurance, and finance. About Dwinity  Dwinity is presently closing its initial seed round at USD 3 MN – funds which will be used for the development of the initial prototypes to hit the market already in 2024. About Nillion The Nillion development company, Nilogy, was incubated by CoinList’s seed program. Nilogy’s Founding CTO was the Founding Engineer of Uber (Conrad Whelan), the Chief Strategy Officer was the Founding CMO of Hedera Hashgraph (Andrew Masanto), the Chief Business Officer is the Founder of Indiegogo (Slava Rubin), the General Counsel was the Associate General Counsel of Coinbase (Lindsay Danas Cohen), along with builders hailing from Consensys, LayerZero, Polygon and Google. “This is a major step on our way to building the leading ecosystem for sensitive data together with one of the top players in the market for the handling of such valuable data. We are looking forward to the cooperation which will add significant trust and competence to both initiatives”, said Peter Koenig, CEO of Dwinity.
en_title: Nillion is pleased to announce that Dwinity, a team pioneering decentralized AI has joined ...
en_content: With huge amounts of personal data being collected every day, privacy concerns are escalating as the impact of data breaches become more costly. The need to address the problems created by the existing data economy have never been greater. By empowering users to reclaim control, Dwinity aims to unlock an enormous amount of potential for personal data to enhance various industries like healthcare, insurance, and finance. About Dwinity Dwinity is presently closing its initial seed round at USD 3 MN – funds which will be used for the development of the initial prototypes to hit the market already in 2024. About Nillion The Nillion development company, Nilogy, was incubated by CoinList’s seed program. Nilogy’s Founding CTO was the Founding Engineer of Uber (Conrad Whelan), the Chief Strategy Officer was the Founding CMO of Hedera Hashgraph (Andrew Masanto), the Chief Business Officer is the Founder of Indiegogo (Slava Rubin), the Generalsens “This is a major step on our way to building the leading ecosystem for sensitive data together with one of the top players in the market for the handling of such valuable data. We are looking forward to the cooperation which will add significant trust and competence to both initiatives", said Peter Koenig, CEO of Dwinity.

title: European Elections: Higher turnout expected, von der Leyen better known than her predecessors
content: Gütersloh, 08.05.2024. 60 percent of Europeans intend to cast their ballots in the European Elections in June. This would be around 10 percent more than in the last elections five years ago. This is the outcome of the new "eupinions" survey conducted by the Bertelsmann Stiftung. The survey also reveals that 75 percent of the European electorate know Ursula von der Leyen by name and face. This means that the current commission president is considerably better known than her predecessors. At the same time, 70 percent of those surveyed indicated that they felt inadequately informed about the commission president's work. "Ursula von der Leyen has achieved something that other commission presidents before her have failed to do: A large majority of Europeans know her by name and also know what she looks like. This is worthy of note, as European politicians have difficulty in gaining media recognition in the member states," says Isabell Hoffmann, Bertelsmann Stiftung Europe specialist and co-author of the current eupinions study – "The Von der Leyen Effect: High visibility, low accountability". However, only 30 percent feel themselves sufficiently informed to be able to assess her performance.    About the Bertelsmann Foundation: Moving people. Shaping the future.  Contact:
en_title: European Elections: Higher turnout expected, von der Leyen better known than her predecessors

This would be around 10 percent more than in the last elections five years ago. This is the outcome of the new "eupinions" survey conducted by the Bertelsmann Stiftung. The survey also reveals that 75 percent of the European electorate know Ursula von der Leyen by name and face. This means that the current commission president is better known than her predecessors. At the same time, 70 percent of those surveyed indicated that they felt indignantly informed about the commission president's work. "Ursula von der Leyen has achieved something that other commission presidents before her have failed to do: A large majority of Europeans know her by name and also know what she looks like. This is worthy of note, as European politicians have difficulty in gaining media recognition in the member states," says Isabell Hoffmann, Bertelsmann Stiftung Europe specialist and co-author of the current eupinions study – "The Von der Leyen Effect: High visibility, low accountability". However, only 30 percent feel adequately informed to be able to assess her performance. About the Bertelsmann Foundation: Moving people. Shaping the future. Contact:

title: element6 Dynamics e PAPACKS® annunciano le loro intenzioni di partnership
content: BOULDER, COLORADO, USA (22 maggio 2024) – element6 Dynamics (https://element6dynamics.com/) e l'azienda tedesca PAPACKS® (https://www.papacks.com/) sono lieti di annunciare la firma di un Memorandum d'Intesa per una joint venture per l'espansione negli Stati Uniti degli imballaggi modellati e termoformati di PAPACKS®, in collaborazione con element6 Dynamics, un pioniere americano della canapa industriale. I dettagli di questo Memorandum d'Intesa sono confidenziali; tuttavia, questa joint venture offrirà soluzioni di imballaggio in fibre alternative rigenerative (canapa industriale) per beni di consumo confezionati, prodotti farmaceutici, tecnologia e aziende generiche che cercano di decarbonizzare, abbandonare la plastica e ridurre la deforestazione nella loro catena di approvvigionamento. "Il nostro obiettivo è fornire nuovi materiali che siano rigenerativi e non estrattivi, e introdurre processi che decarbonizzino e producano poco o nessun rifiuto a prezzi competitivi rispetto alle soluzioni esistenti", ha dichiarato Kimberly Kovacs, CEO di element6 Dynamics e ex onorata di Forbes 50 over 50 Women in Finance. "PAPACKS® è un leader innovativo nei materiali a base di fibre, focalizzato su soluzioni sostenibili, e siamo entusiasti di collaborare con loro per portare questa joint venture negli Stati Uniti." element6 Dynamics è un leader statunitense nella canapa industriale, comprese ma non limitate alla riproduzione genetica, coltivazione e conversione della biomassa di canapa industriale (per imitare le caratteristiche della polpa kraft di legno tenero tradizionale). La polpa di element6 Dynamics (e6 Pulp) si è dimostrata una soluzione di polpa pronta per il mercato che può essere integrata nelle infrastrutture di cartiere e imballaggi nuove o esistenti. PAPACKS® ha stabilito lo standard in Europa per gli imballaggi sostenibili a base di fibre, soddisfacendo le esigenze specifiche di imballaggi alimentari e non alimentari, farmaceutici e cosmetici. L'azienda dà priorità all'uso di materie prime rinnovabili nella ricerca e nella produzione, comprese le nuove fibre e la canapa industriale. Il loro impegno per l'innovazione è riconosciuto a livello mondiale attraverso oltre 75 famiglie di brevetti, 30 premi e più di un decennio di lavoro pionieristico. Le parti intendono costruire 10 impianti di produzione modellati e termoformati completamente integrati negli Stati Uniti, adottando un approccio bio-regionale posizionando ciascun impianto vicino alle loro aree di coltivazione e ai partenariati accademici, garantendo una soluzione economica ed ecologica per rilocalizzare le attività aziendali in modo sostenibile e responsabile. "Crediamo che element6 Dynamics sarà un forte partner finanziario e strategico per noi negli Stati Uniti", ha dichiarato Tahsin Dag, CEO di PAPACKS®, che è stato eletto "CEO dell'anno 2024 nell'industria degli imballaggi" dagli European CEO Awards. "Il team di element6 Dynamics ha raggiunto con successo la scala necessaria per soddisfare la nostra base di clienti internazionale. La combinazione delle nostre rispettive competenze, conoscenze, tecnologie e visioni condivise posiziona la nostra partnership per il successo." Informazioni su element6 Dynamics  Il nostro impegno per la protezione dell'ambiente e la costruzione di aziende sostenibili e orientate al futuro è radicato nelle nostre operazioni perché crediamo che un'azienda possa prosperare facendo del bene. Concentrandoci sulla circolarità nei nostri processi, contribuiamo a un futuro senza rifiuti e facilitiamo l'adozione diffusa di pratiche rigenerative. Informazioni su PAPACKS®  Contatto stampa / Richieste
en_title: element6 Dynamics and PAPACKS® announce their partnership intentions
en_content: BOULDER, COLORADO, USA (May 22, 2024) – element6 Dynamics (https://element6dynamics.com/) and the German company PAPACKS® (https://www.papacks.com/) are pleased to announce the signature of a Memorandum d'Intesa for a joint venture for the expansion in the United States of molded and thermoformed packaging of PAPACKS® The details of this Memorandum of Understanding are confidential; However, this joint venture will offer alternative regenerative fiber packaging solutions (industrial hemp) for packaged consumer goods, pharmaceutical products, technology and generic companies that try to decarbonize, abandon plastic and reduce deforestation in their supply chain. "Our goal is to provide new materials that are regenerative and not extractive, and to introduce processes that decarbonize and produce little or no waste at competitive prices compared to existing solutions," said Kimberly Kovacs, CEO of element6 Dynamics and former honor of Forbes 50 over 50 Women in Finance. "PAPACKS® is an innovative leader in fiber-based materials, focused on sustainable solutions, and we are excited to collaborate with them to bring this joint venture to the United States." Element6 Dynamics is an American leader in industrial hemp, including but not limited to genetic reproduction, and conversion of industrial hemp biomass (to imitate the characteristics of traditional softwood pulp). Element6 Dynamics pulp (e6 Pulp) proved to be a ready-to-market pulp solution that can be integrated into new or existing paper mill infrastructures and packaging. PAPACKS® has established the standard in Europe for sustainable fibre-based packaging, meeting the specific requirements of food and non-food packaging, pharmaceuticals and cosmetics. The company gives priority to the use of renewable raw materials in research and production, including new fibers and industrial hemp. Their commitment to innovation is recognised worldwide through over 75 patent families, 30 awards and more than a decade of pioneering work. The parties intend to build 10 fully integrated modeled and thermoformed production plants in the United States, adopting a bio-regional approach by placing each plant close to their cultivation areas and academic partnerships, ensuring an economic and ecological solution to relocate business activities in a sustainable and responsible manner. "We believe that element6 Dynamics will be a strong financial and strategic partner for us in the United States," said Tahsin Dag, CEO of PAPACKS®, who was elected "CEO of the year 2024 in the packaging industry" by the European CEO Awards. "The element6 Dynamics team has successfully reached the scale needed to meet our international customer base. The combination of our respective skills, knowledge, technologies and shared visions places our partnership for success." Information about element6 Dynamics Our commitment to environmental protection and the construction of sustainable and future-oriented companies is rooted in our operations because we believe that a company can thrive doing good. Focusing on circularity in our processes, we contribute to a future without waste and facilitate the widespread adoption of regenerative practices. About PAPACKS® Press Contact / Requests

title: Armando Capone è il nuovo Chief Executive Officer di Experian Italia
content: Negli ultimi anni, Experian ha abbracciato sempre più l’innovazione dai dati e dagli analytics, grazie anche al supporto crescente di un ecosistema di partner qualificati, andando a implementare tecnologie e servizi all’avanguardia come Analytics as Service, Machine Learning, Cloud Decisioning e Generative AI con l’obiettivo di comprendere la sempre crescente mole di dati generati da aziende e consumatori e ridefinire continuamente il proprio ruolo in un panorama del credito in costante evoluzione. Oggi Experian è una forza globale con una presenza in oltre 30 Paesi, forte di un team di 22.000 professionisti dedicati a sfruttare il potere dei dati e degli analytics per generare valore significativo per aziende, società e singoli consumatori di tutto il mondo. Armando Capone è entrato in Experian nel 2016, ricoprendo inizialmente il ruolo di Senior Business Consultant nell’EMEA Global Consulting Practice, seguendo progetti di consulenza in Italia ed in Europa e concentrandosi sulle tematiche relative alla Regulation. A partire dal 2020 ha ricoperto il ruolo di Chief Commercial Officer, con responsabilità sulle attività commerciali dell’azienda a livello italiano, mentre dal 2022 è diventato General Manager. Prima di entrare in Experian, aveva maturato un’esperienza più che decennale in Accenture nell'ambito delle aree Strategy e Finance & Risk.
en_title: Armando Capone is the new Chief Executive Officer of Experian Italia
en_content: In recent years, Experian has increasingly embraced innovation from data and analytics, thanks also to the growing support of a qualified partner ecosystem, implementing cutting-edge technologies and services such as Analytics as Service, Machine Learning, Cloud Decisioning and Generative AI with the aim of understanding the growing amount of data generated by companies and consumers and continually redefining its role in a constantly evolving credit landscape. Today Experian is a global force with a presence in more than 30 countries, a strong team of 22,000 professionals dedicated to exploiting the power of data and analytics to generate significant value for companies, companies and single consumers worldwide. Armando Capone joined Experian in 2016, initially covering the role of Senior Business Consultant in the EMEA Global Consulting Practice, following consulting projects in Italy and Europe and focusing on Regulation issues. Since 2020 he has served as Chief Commercial Officer, with responsibility for the business activities of the company at the Italian level, while since 2022 he has become General Manager. Before entering Experian, he had gained more than ten years experience in Accenture in Strategy and Finance & Risk areas.

title: Record sales and EBITA achieved:  Global SME sector trusts NTT DATA Business Solutions
content: Bielefeld-based SAP consultancy marks a milestone in its history as result (EBITA) exceeds EUR 100 million for the first time  “I’m delighted that, in our 35th anniversary year, we were able to build seamlessly on the excellent results of the previous fiscal year,” said Norbert Rotter, CEO of NTT DATA Business Solutions AG and EVP of NTT DATA, Inc. “Under the umbrella of Japan-based NTT DATA Group, we are continuing to develop successfully into a globally recognized IT service provider. Our customers value our extensive SAP expertise and our complementary offering of ServiceNow or Microsoft. As a leading global SAP partner for the SME sector, we are the right choice when it comes to transforming companies into Intelligent Enterprises.” The current figures also reflect this: In the previous fiscal year, NTT DATA Business Solutions was more successful than ever before, especially in international business. As part of ongoing internationalization, foreign sales as a proportion of total sales increased further from just over 50 percent in 2017 to 57.6 percent in the current reporting period, moving closer towards the 60 percent mark. Sales grew particularly strongly in Western Europe (+18.4% to EUR 227.9 million) and in Northern and Eastern Europe (+23.9% to EUR 316.3 million). In its home market of Germany/Austria/Switzerland, NTT DATA Business Solutions likewise saw a double-digit increase of just over 10 percent to EUR 684.3 million. The company also recorded growth in the Americas (+9.6% to EUR 275.6 million) and APAC (+4.8% to EUR 99.6 million). In addition, earnings before interest, taxes, and amortization (EBITA) exceeded the three-digit million mark for the first time and climbed by 4.2 percent to the historic high of EUR 103.4 million, which equates to an EBITA margin of 6.4 percent. On an adjusted basis, EBITA was EUR 118.8 million, meaning that an adjusted EBITA margin of 7.4 percent was achieved – a further increase on the previous year’s record level of 7.0 percent. The adjustments in the order of EUR 15.4 million are based on the parent company’s management fees, which are substantially higher than in the previous year, and on significant acquisition and integration costs for Sapphire Systems. These figures, together with the doubling of the workforce to 15,300 since 2017, mean that the 2023/24 fiscal year is the most successful in the company’s history to date. An absolute driver of growth was once again the cloud subscription sales segment. Here, sales increased by 42.7 percent to EUR 128.4 million. This segment had also performed very well in the previous year with growth of 57.1 percent. Overall, this area has more than doubled in the past two years. In line with this development, licensing business fell by -27 percent, while consulting business and managed services exceeded the previous year’s figures, at EUR 770.3 million (+18.2%) and EUR 674.7 million (+7.8%) respectively.  “As a SAP Global Strategic Service Partner, NTT DATA Group has significantly exceeded all sales targets with SAP products in the past fiscal year, particularly in the cloud sector. At NTT DATA Business Solutions, we expect to see continued strong growth in this area and will take an ambitious approach to pursuing our sales targets,” said Norbert Rotter. “Artificial intelligence is another important field of innovation for us. Our SME customers are increasingly using AI solutions in their transformation, which is why we will continue to drive forward this topic based on SAP for them.” In addition to its core business with SAP, the Bielefeld-based company is increasingly focusing on complementary applications. NTT DATA Business Solutions took a major step in this direction last year with the acquisition of Sapphire Systems. Via partnerships with SAP, ServiceNow, Infor SunSystems, HxGN and AWS, the British company provides digital operations software and services for SME customers in the US and UK. The acquisition underscores NTT DATA Business Solutions’ efforts to reinforce its position as a leading SAP partner for the SME sector and to position itself as a principal provider both in the SAP SI market for large customers and for advanced digitalization solutions for SME customers. Further acquisitions are planned for the new fiscal year; however, the company does not wish to comment on these at this time. Jürgen Pürzer, Chief Financial Officer at NTT DATA Business Solutions AG, is optimistic about the future: “After two record years, we don’t want to take a break. We’re committed to consistently pursuing our growth and earnings targets. We expect sales growth of up to six percent on a comparable basis. To achieve this, we will continue to work with a global cost and efficiency program, invest consciously on behalf of our customers, and offer a compelling range of comprehensive services and products.” Further information is available at  NTT DATA Business Solutions is part of  Financial contact Katrin Schlegel Press contact

An absolute driver of growth was once again the cloud subscription sales segment. Here, sales increased by 42.7 percent to EUR 128.4 million. This segment also performed very well in the previous year with growth of 57.1 percent. Overall, this area has more than doubled in the past two years. In line with this development, licensing business fell by -27 percent, while consulting business and managed services exceeded the previous year’s figures, at EUR 770.3 million (+18.2%) and EUR 674.7 million (+7.8%) equivalent.  “As a SAP Global Strategic Service Partner, NTT DATA Group has significantly exceeded all sales targets with SAP products in the past fiscal year, particularly in the cloud sector. At NTT DATA Business Solutions, we expect to see continued strong growth in this area and will take an ambitious approach to our sales targets,” said Norbert Rotter. “Artificial intelligence is another important field of innovation for us. Our SME customers are growing using AI solutions in their transformation, which is why we will continue to drive forward this topic based on SAP for them.” In addition to its core business with SAP, the Bielefeld-based company is growing focusing on complementary applications. NTT DATA Business Solutions took a major step in this direction last year with the acquisition of Sapphire Systems. Via partnerships with SAP, ServiceNow, Infor SunSystems, HxGN and AWS, the British company provides digital operations software and services for SME customers in the US and UK. The acquisition underscores NTT DATA Business Solutions’ efforts to reinforce its position as a leading SAP partner for the SME sector and to position itself as a principal provider both in the SAP SI market for large customers and for advanced digitalization solutions for SME customers. Further acquisitions are planned for the new fiscal year; however, the company does not wish to comment on these at this time. Jürgen Pürzer, Chief Financial Officer at NTT DATA Business Solutions AG, is optimistic about the future: “After two record years, we don’t want to take a break. We’re committed to assert our growth and earnings targets. We expect sales growth of up to six percent on a similar basis. To achieve this, we will continue to work with a global cost and efficiency program, invest consciously on behalf of our customers, and offer a complling range of comprehensive services and products.” Further information is available at NTT DATA Business Solutions is part of Financial contact Katrin Schlegel Press contact

title: Wipro espande l’offerta di canali di vendita al dettaglio in collaborazione con Cisco e AT&T
content: Wipro Limited (NYSE: WIT, BSE: 507685, NSE: WIPRO), una delle principali società di consulenza e servizi tecnologici, oggi ha annunciato l’espansione delle funzionalità focalizzate sulla vendita al dettaglio nell’ambito di Wipro VisionEDGE+. Questa offerta potenziata, sviluppata tramite una partnership stretta con Cisco e AT&T e che utilizza Amazon Web Services (AWS), serve da piattaforma di trasformazione completa nel settore retail, progettata per liberare l’intero potenziale della rete di canali per le vendite al dettaglio. title: Charge Fast, Pay less: IONITY lowers Prices and Introduces Tailored Tariffs
content: Munich, Germany, May 27, 2024 – IONITY is making High Power Charging (HPC) with up to 350 kW affordable for everyone. The leading pan-European HPC network for electric vehicles (EVs) of all brands is introducing country-specific tariffs to flexibly cater to its customers’ charging needs. From May 28, IONITY is lowering IONITY DIRECT charging prices in 12 European countries. Additionally, IONITY introduces two new tariff plans with flexible terms tailored to customers' varying mileage. IONITY PASSPORT MOTION and IONITY PASSPORT POWER offer significant price reductions compared to ad-hoc charging. Depending on the country and the tariff, prices per kilowatt hour are available from 0,26 EUR. With the new PASSPORT tariffs, IONITY offers tailor-made, cost-efficient, ultra-fast charging options for long-distance business and leisure trips across Europe, as well as for daily use.   Tariffs at a glance  Why choose IONITY? * The monthly basic fee depends on the country (and local currency) in which the customer is resident. Discount in the first month applies only to the initial subscription of either the IONITY PASSPORT MOTION or IONITY PASSPORT POWER tariffs. This discount does not apply to customers who switch between IONITY PASSPORT MOTION and IONITY PASSPORT POWER. The actual price per kWh depends on the country (and local currency) where the EV is charged. About IONITY Follow us: Press contact fischerAppelt, relations GmbH

title: Bihar collabora con Bidgely per ridurre i furti di energia e le perdite di ricavi tramite l'analisi dei dati alimentata dall'IA
content: Questo comunicato stampa include contenuti multimediali. Visualizzare l’intero comunicato qui: Bidgely's AI technology helps BSPHCL utilize smart meter data to detect energy theft faster and with greater precision. (Graphic: Business Wire) La tecnologia dell'IA di Bidgely, che analizza i dati dei contatori intelligenti per evidenziare comportamenti e modelli anomali nell'utilizzo dell'energia da parte dei consumatori, ha già segnalato oltre 136 potenziali casi di furto. Tutti i casi sono stati ispezionati ed è stata emessa una valutazione in quasi il 63% dei casi di abuso delle tariffe. Per quanto riguarda il bypass dei contatori e il furto notturno, anche se nel 51% degli indizi è risultato corretto, sono state effettuate prenotazioni nel 41% dei casi segnalati. Complessivamente, sono stati registrati il 57 percento dei casi, il tutto entro un solo mese dall'utilizzo. BSPHCL è anche la prima utility statale in India a utilizzare l'intelligenza artificiale avanzata per mitigare le perdite commerciali e quelle legate ai furti. “Investire nei contatori intelligenti è stato il primo passo per modernizzare la nostra comprensione del consumo energetico. Ora stiamo sfruttando l'analisi dei dati di IA/ML che ci porterà a un livello superiore per gestire diversi casi d'uso che sfruttano i dati dei contatori intelligenti, come la perdita di entrate, la previsione della domanda, la contabilità energetica, la pianificazione della rete di distribuzione per gli asset di rete e altro ancora, senza dipendere da hardware di monitoraggio aggiuntivo o dall'indicizzazione dei consumatori. È qui che stiamo collaborando con fornitori di soluzioni come Bidgely per fornire soluzioni di perdita di ricavi e per sbloccare il valore dell'analisi dei dati guidata dall'IA/ML per il Bihar", ha dichiarato Sanjeev Hans, CMD di Bihar State Power Holding Company Limited. Invece di individuare i furti dall'alto verso il basso, dall'alimentatore al trasformatore e infine al livello del cliente, l'intelligenza artificiale di Bidgely è in grado di elaborare i dati a livello individuale a un ritmo più rapido per rivelare modelli anomali coerenti con il bypass parziale o completo del contatore, il bypass di apparecchi pesanti come l'aria condizionata, il furto notturno, la manomissione del contatore e l'abuso della tariffa, garantendo così la protezione dei ricavi. Laddove gli approcci tradizionali al rilevamento dei furti richiedono anche un massiccio intervento manuale, l'intelligenza artificiale assume questo ruolo per rilevare le perdite in una frazione di tempo. Bidgely ha dimostrato per la prima volta la sua soluzione per il furto di energia in occasione della fiera indiana “I furti di energia rappresentano quasi il 20-30% di perdita di entrate per le aziende statali indiane, ma le attuali strategie per bloccare le perdite e contabilizzare i furti danno in media solo il 10-20% di risultati”, ha dichiarato Abhay Gupta, CEO di Bidgely. Invece di affidarsi esclusivamente all'intelligence locale e alle ispezioni in loco, BSPHCL può ora sfruttare le tecnologie di intelligenza artificiale per individuare i furti di energia in modo più rapido e preciso”. Oltre ai risparmi sui ricavi, le ore di lavoro risparmiate possono anche favorire il miglioramento dei servizi per i consumatori”. Per saperne di più sulla Soluzione Furto di Energia di Bidgely, che consente di rilevare l'uso improprio di energia e di migliorare la gestione dei processi interni, visitate il sito  Bidgely è un'azienda SaaS basata sull'intelligenza artificiale che velocizza un futuro con energia pulita consentendo a fornitori di energia e a consumatori di assumere decisioni correlate all'energia basandosi su dati. Basata sull'esclusiva tecnologia brevettata dell'azienda, la piattaforma UtilityAI™ di Bidgely trasforma dimensioni multiple dei dati dei clienti, come consumo energetico, dati demografici e interazioni, in approfondite informazioni estremamente accurate e fruibili sull'energia utilizzata dagli utenti. La società attinge a tali informazioni per fornire consigli personalizzati a ogni singolo cliente, in base alla personalità e allo stile di vita specifici, agli attributi d'utilizzo, ai modelli comportamentali, alla propensione all'acquisto e ad altri criteri ancora. Dal punto di vista delle risorse energetiche distribuite (DER, Distributed Energy Resources) e della prospettiva del Grid Edge, Bidgely promuove l'innovazione dei contatori intelligenti con soluzioni basate sui dati per fotovoltaico solare, rilevamento dei veicoli elettrici, spostamento del carico comportamentale degli EV e ricarica gestita, furti di energia, previsioni del carico a breve termine, analisi della rete e progettazione delle tariffe TOU. L'analisi energetica di UtilityAI™ targata Bidgely garantisce un'approfondita visuale sulla generazione e sul consumo, per una miglior gestione dei picchi di carico e per la pianificazione della rete, oltre a fornire consigli mirati su nuovi prodotti e servizi a valore aggiunto. Con radici nella Silicon Valley, Bidgely vanta oltre 16 brevetti nel settore dell'energia e finanziamenti che superano i 75 milioni di dollari USA, collabora da tempo con più di 30 data scientist e porta la passione per l'IA ai fornitori di servizi di pubblica utilità per clienti residenziali e commerciali in tutto il mondo. Per ulteriori informazioni visitare il sito Il testo originale del presente annuncio, redatto nella lingua di partenza, è la versione ufficiale che fa fede. Le traduzioni sono offerte unicamente per comodità del lettore e devono rinviare al testo in lingua originale, che è l'unico giuridicamente valido.   Christine Bennett
en_title: Bihar collaborates with Bidgely to reduce energy thefts and revenue losses through data analysis powered by AI
en_content: This press release includes multimedia content. View the entire release here: Bidgely's AI technology helps BSPHCL uses smart meter data to detect energy theft faster and with greater precision. (Graphic: Business Wire) Bidgely's AI technology, which analyzes data from smart meters to highlight abnormal behaviors and patterns in the use of energy by consumers, has already reported over 136 potential cases of theft. All cases have been inspected and an assessment has been made in almost 63% of the cases of tariff abuse. With regard to the bypass of the meters and the night theft, although in 51% of the clues it was correct, reservations were made in 41% of the reported cases. Overall, 57 percent of cases were recorded, all within one month of use. BSPHCL is also the first state utility in India to use advanced artificial intelligence to mitigate commercial losses and those related to theft. “In investing in smart meters was the first step to modernize our understanding of energy consumption. We are now taking advantage of IA/ML data analysis that will lead us to a higher level to manage different usage cases that leverage data from smart meters, such as loss of revenue, demand forecasting, energy accounting, distribution network planning for network assets and more, without depending on additional monitoring hardware or consumer indexing. This is where we are collaborating with solutions providers like Bidgely to provide revenue loss solutions and to unlock the value of AI/ML data analysis for Bihar," said Sanjeev Hans, CMD of Bihar State Power Holding Company Limited. Instead of identifying thefts from above to bottom, from the power supply to the transformer and finally to the level of the customer, Bidgely's artificial intelligence is able to process the data at an individual level at a faster rate to reveal abnormal models consistent with the partial or complete bypass of the counter, the bypass of heavy appliances such as air conditioning, night theft, the tampering of the counter and thus the abuse of the tariff. Where traditional approaches to the detection of thefts also require massive manual intervention, artificial intelligence assumes this role to detect losses in a fraction of time. Bidgely has demonstrated for the first time its solution for energy theft at the Indian trade fair “Energy thefts represent almost 20-30% of revenue loss for Indian state companies, but current strategies to block losses and account for thefts give on average only 10-20% results”, said Abhay Gupta, CEO of Bidgely. Instead of relying solely on local intelligence and on-site inspections, BSPHCL can now use artificial intelligence technologies to detect energy thefts faster and more precisely.” In addition to revenue savings, spared hours of work can also help improve consumer services.” To learn more about Bidgely's Energy Fur Solution, which allows you to detect misuse of energy and improve internal process management, visit Bidgely website is an AI-based SaaS company that speeds up a future with clean energy allowing energy suppliers and consumers to take energy-related decisions based on data. Based on the company's exclusive patented technology, Bidgely's UtilityAITM platform transforms multiple dimensions of customer data, such as energy consumption, demographic data and interactions, in-depth information that is extremely accurate and accessible to users. The company draws on this information to provide personalized advice to each individual customer, based on the specific personality and lifestyle, attributes of use, behavioral models, propensity to purchase and other criteria yet. From the point of view of distributed energy resources (DER, Distributed Energy Resources) and Grid Edge perspective, Bidgely promotes the innovation of intelligent meters with data-based solutions for solar photovoltaics, detection of electric vehicles, shift of behavioral load of EV and managed charging, energy thefts, short-term load forecasts, network analysis and TOU pricing. The energy analysis of UtilityAITM licensed Bidgely guarantees an in-depth view of generation and consumption, for better management of loading peaks and network planning, as well as providing targeted advice on new products and value-added services. With roots in the Silicon Valley, Bidgely has over 16 energy patents and funding that exceed 75 million US dollars, has long been collaborating with more than 30 data scientists and brings the passion for AI to public utility service providers for residential and commercial customers worldwide. For more information visit the site The original text of this announcement, written in the starting language, is the official version that makes faith. The translations are offered only for convenience of the reader and must refer to the text in the original language, which is the only legally valid. Christine Bennett

title: Takeda and Pfizer Announce Four-Year Results from Positive Phase 3 HD21 Trial of Additional ADCETRIS® (brentuximab vedotin) Combination in Frontline Hodgkin Lymphoma
content: Takeda The HD21 study is a Phase 3, randomized, multi-country, prospective, open-label study, sponsored by the GHSG and supported by Takeda, designed to evaluate ADCETRIS in combination with etoposide, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, dacarbazine and dexamethasone (BrECADD) in comparison to a standard of care treatment – escalated doses of bleomycin, etoposide, doxorubicin, cyclophosphamide, vincristine, procarbazine, prednisone (eBEACOPP) – in patients with newly diagnosed Stage IIb/III/IV classical Hodgkin lymphoma. At a preplanned three-year analysis, the study met its co-primary endpoints, with the ADCETRIS combination regimen demonstrating significantly improved safety as assessed by treatment-related morbidity (TRMB) and non-inferior PFS. The study found that the addition of ADCETRIS to this chemotherapy regimen improved the risk-to-benefit profile of the combination treatment, maintaining efficacy with significantly fewer acute and long-lasting treatment-related toxicities than the comparator arm. “We initiated the HD21 trial with the hope of improving outcomes currently being achieved by a standard of care, as many patients with newly diagnosed disease often experience a high treatment burden,” said Peter Borchmann, MD, PhD, University Hospital of Cologne, Germany, and trial chairman of the HD21 study. “The presented analysis, in which the ADCETRIS regimen demonstrates superior progression-free survival, as well as a tolerable safety profile, reveals the meaningful potential this ADCETRIS + ECADD regimen has to offer these patients.” The ASCO presentation provides details of a four-year PFS analysis of the HD21 study conducted by GHSG. After 48 months, BrECADD showed superior efficacy to BEACOPP (94.3% PFS for BrECADD and 90.9% PFS for eBEACOPP; hazard ratio [HR]: 0.66 [95% CI:88.7-93.1]; p<0.035). As previously reported in the three-year analysis, treatment with BrECADD was also associated with a significant reduction in the incidence of TRMB compared with BEACOPP (n=738; 42% vs 59%; p<0.001), as well as clinically meaningful reductions in adverse events (AEs). The safety profile of ADCETRIS in patients receiving BrECADD remained consistent with other approved ADCETRIS combination regimens, and no new safety signals were identified. “In our ongoing effort to improve outcomes for patients with lymphoma, we’ve partnered with the GHSG on the HD21 study to deepen our understanding of how ADCETRIS could further benefit patients in need of new options,” said Awny Farajallah, chief medical officer, global oncology at Takeda. “We are excited about the impact these results could have on patients with newly diagnosed Hodgkin lymphoma, potentially bringing them an additional ADCETRIS-based combination regimen that may significantly reduce side effects without compromising on efficacy.” Takeda will be responsible for submission of potential regulatory filings based on the HD21 study outside of the U.S. and Canada. Under the terms of the collaboration agreement, Pfizer has U.S. and Canadian commercialization rights and Takeda has rights to commercialize ADCETRIS in the rest of the world.  Enrolled patients with newly diagnosed, Stage IIb with large mediastinal mass and/or extranodal lesions, Stage III or IV Hodgkin lymphoma were randomized to receive two cycles of either escalated BEACOPP or BrECADD, respectively, followed by interim PET staging. A decision is then made if patients received a further two or four cycles of escalated BEACOPP or BrECADD. The HD21 trial aims to evaluate a modified treatment regimen to minimize side effects, while maintaining similar responses to treatment. The study has co-primary endpoints: safety is assessed by treatment-related morbidity (TRMB) (superiority), a novel endpoint focused on clinically relevant, acute toxicities of primary chemotherapy, and efficacy is assessed by PFS (non-inferiority). Secondary endpoints are tumor response (complete response [CR] rate), overall survival (OS), infertility rate at one year, second malignancies, frequency of adverse events, therapy adherence and quality of life.  According to the International Agency for Research on Cancer, in 2020, over 83,000 people worldwide were diagnosed with Hodgkin lymphoma and approximately 23,000 people died from this cancer.  ADCETRIS injection for intravenous infusion has received FDA approval for seven indications: Health Canada granted ADCETRIS approval with conditions for relapsed or refractory Hodgkin lymphoma and sALCL in 2013, and non-conditional approval for post-autologous stem cell transplantation (ASCT) consolidation treatment of Hodgkin lymphoma patients at increased risk of relapse or progression in 2017, adults with pcALCL or CD30-expressing MF who have had prior systemic therapy in 2018, for previously untreated Stage IV Hodgkin lymphoma in combination with doxorubicin, vinblastine, and dacarbazine in 2019, and for previously untreated adult patients with sALCL, peripheral T-cell lymphoma-not otherwise specified (PTCL-NOS) or angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma (AITL), whose tumors express CD30, in combination with cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, prednisone in 2019. ADCETRIS received conditional marketing authorization from the European Commission in October 2012, and the specific obligations of the conditional marketing authorization were fulfilled in May 2022. The approved indications in the European Union are: (1) for the treatment of adult patients with previously untreated CD30-positive Stage III & IV Hodgkin lymphoma in combination with doxorubicin, vinblastine and dacarbazine (AVD), (2) for the treatment of adult patients with CD30-positive Hodgkin lymphoma at increased risk of relapse or progression following ASCT, (3) for the treatment of adult patients with relapsed or refractory CD30-positive Hodgkin lymphoma following ASCT, or following at least two prior therapies when ASCT or multi-agent chemotherapy is not a treatment option, (4) for the treatment of adult patients with relapsed or refractory sALCL, (5) for the treatment of adult patients with previously untreated sALCL in combination with CHP and (6) for the treatment of adult patients with CD30-positive cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (CTCL) after at least one prior systemic therapy. ADCETRIS has received marketing authorization by regulatory authorities in more than 70 countries for relapsed or refractory Hodgkin lymphoma and sALCL. See Important Safety Information below. ADCETRIS is being evaluated broadly in more than 70 clinical trials, including a Phase 3 study in first-line Hodgkin lymphoma (ECHELON-1) and another Phase 3 study in first-line CD30-positive peripheral T-cell lymphomas (ECHELON-2), as well as trials in many additional types of CD30-positive malignancies. Pfizer and Takeda fund joint development costs for ADCETRIS on a 50:50 basis, except in Japan where Takeda is solely responsible for development costs.    Closely monitor patients for new or worsening neurological, cognitive, or behavioral signs or symptoms, which may be suggestive of PML. Suggested evaluation of PML includes neurology consultation, gadolinium-enhanced magnetic resonance imaging of the brain, and cerebrospinal fluid analysis for JCV DNA by polymerase chain reaction or a brain biopsy with evidence of JCV. A negative JCV PCR does not exclude PML. Additional follow-up and evaluation may be warranted if no alternative diagnosis can be established. Hold dosing for any suspected case of PML and permanently discontinue ADCETRIS if a diagnosis of PML is confirmed. Be alert to PML symptoms that the patient may not notice (e.g., cognitive, neurological, or psychiatric symptoms).         When ADCETRIS is administered in combination with AVD or CHP, primary prophylaxis with G-CSF is recommended for all patients beginning with the first dose.                         Administer G-CSF primary prophylaxis beginning with Cycle 1 for adult patients who receive ADCETRIS in combination with chemotherapy for previously untreated Stage III/IV cHL or previously untreated PTCL, and pediatric patients who receive ADCETRIS in combination with chemotherapy for previously untreated high risk cHL. Monitor complete blood counts prior to each ADCETRIS dose. Monitor more frequently for patients with Grade 3 or 4 neutropenia. Monitor patients for fever. If Grade 3 or 4 neutropenia develops, consider dose delays, reductions, discontinuation, or G-CSF prophylaxis with subsequent doses.                       The information contained in this release is as of June 1, 2024. Pfizer assumes no obligation to update forward-looking statements contained in this release as the result of new information or future events or developments. Risks and uncertainties include, among other things, uncertainties regarding the commercial success of ADCETRIS; the uncertainties inherent in research and development, including the ability to meet anticipated clinical endpoints, commencement and/or completion dates for our clinical trials, regulatory submission dates, regulatory approval dates and/or launch dates, as well as the possibility of unfavorable new clinical data and further analyses of existing clinical data; the risk that clinical trial data are subject to differing interpretations and assessments by regulatory authorities; whether regulatory authorities will be satisfied with the design of and results from our clinical studies; whether and when drug applications may be filed in particular jurisdictions for ADCETRIS with eCADD or as a single agent for any potential indication; whether and when any applications that may be pending or filed for ADCETRIS, may be approved by regulatory authorities, which will depend on myriad factors, including making a determination as to whether the product's benefits outweigh its known risks and determination of the product's efficacy and, if approved, whether ADCETRIS with eCADD or as a single agent will be commercially successful; decisions by regulatory authorities impacting labeling, manufacturing processes, safety and/or other matters that could affect the availability or commercial potential of ADCETRIS; uncertainties regarding the impact of COVID-19 on Pfizer’s business, operations and financial results; and competitive developments. en_title: Takeda and Pfizer Announce Four-Year Results from Positive Phase 3 HD21 Trial of Additional ADCETRIS® (brentuximab Vedotin) Combination in Frontline Hodgkin Lymphoma
en_content: The HD21 study is a Phase 3, randomized, multi-country, prospective, open-label study, sponsored by the GHSG and supported by Takeda, designed to evaluate ADCETRIS in combination with etoposide, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, dacarbazine and dexamethasone (BrECADD) in comparison to standard of care At a preplanned three-year analysis, the study met its co-primary endpoints, with the ADCETRIS combination regimen demonstrating significantly improved safety as assessed by treatment-related softty (TRMB) and non-inferior PFS. The study found that the addition of ADCETRIS to this chemotherapy regimen improved the risk-to-benefit profile of the combination treatment, maintaining efficacy with significantly fewer acute and long-lasting treatment-related toxicities than the comparator arm. “We launched the HD21 trial with the hope of improving outcomes currently being achieved by a standard of care, as many patients with newly diagnosed disease experience a high treatment burden,” said Peter Borchmann, MD, PhD, University Hospital of Cologne, Germany, and trial chairman of the HD21 study. “The presented analysis, in which the ADCETRIS regimen demonstrats superior progression-free survival, as well as a tolerable safety profile, reveals the significant potential this ADCETRIS + ECADD regimen has to offer these patients.” The ASCO provides details of a four-year PFS analysis of the HD21 study conducted by GHSG. After 48 months, BrECADD showed superior efficacy to BEACOPP (94.3% PFS for BrECADD and 90.9% PFS for eBEACOPP; hazard ratio [HR]: 0.66 [95% CI:88.7-93.1]; p<0.035). As previously reported in the three-year analysis, treatment with BrECADD was also associated with a significant reduction in the incidence of TRMB compared with BEACOPP (n=738; 42% vs 59%; p<0.001), as well as clinically significant reductions in adverse events (AEs). The safety profile of ADCETRIS in patients receiving BrECADD remained consistent with other approved ADCETRIS combination regimens, and no new safety signals were identified. “In our ongoing effort to improve outcomes for patients with lymphoma, we’ve partnered with the GHSG on the HD21 study to deepen our understanding of how ADCETRIS could further benefits patients in need of new options,” said Awny Farajallah, chief medical officer, global oncology at Takeda. “We are excited about the impact these results could have on patients with newly diagnosed Hodgkin lymphoma, potentially bringing them an additional ADCETRIS-based combination regimen that may significantly reduce side effects without compromising on efficacy.” Takeda will be responsible for submission of potential regulatory filings based on the HD21 study outside of the U.S. and Canada. Under the terms of the collaboration agreement, Pfizer has U.S. and Canadian commercialization rights and Takeda has rights to commercialize ADCETRIS in the rest of the world. Enrolled patients with newly diagnosed, Stage IIb with large mediastinal mass and/or extranodal lesions, Stage III or IV Hodgkin lymphoma were randomized to receive two cycles of either escalated BEACOPP or BrECADD, respectively, followed by interim PET staging. A decision is then made if patients received a further two or four cycles of escalated BEACOPP or BrECADD. The HD21 trial aims to assess a modified treatment regimen to minimize side effects, while maintaining similar responses to treatment. The study has co-primary endpoints: safety is assessed by treatment-related softty (TRMB) (superiority), a novel endpoint focused on clinically relevant, acute toxicities of primary chemotherapy, and efficacy is assessed by PFS (non-inferiority). Secondary endpoints are tumor response (complete response [CR] rate), overall survival (OS), infertility rate at one year, second malignancies, frequency of adverse events, therapy adherence and quality of life. According to the International Agency for Research on Cancer, in 2020, over 83,000 people worldwide were diagnosed with Hodgkin lymphoma and approximately 23,000 people died from this cancer. Pfizer and Takeda fund joint development costs for ADCETRIS on a 50:50 basis, except in Japan where Takeda is solely responsible for development costs. A negative JCV PCR does not exclude PML. Additional follow-up and evaluation may be warranted if no alternative diagnosis can be established. Hold dosing for any suspected case of PML and permanently discontinue ADCETRIS if a diagnosis of PML is confirmed. Be alert to PML symptoms that the patient may not notice (e.g., cognitive, neurological, or psychiatric symptoms). When ADCETRIS is administered in combination with AVD or CHP, primary prophylaxis with G-CSF is recommended for all patients beginning with the first dose. title: Caro affitti, giovani pensano a coabitare
subtitle: Fond. Feltrinelli e PoliTO, anche come soluzione per la vita
content:
Di fronte al caro vita e ai prezzi
altissimi dell'Immobiliare i giovani, comunque gli under 40,
prendono ormai sempre più in considerazione la soluzione della
coabitazione 'fluida'. Quello che una volta veniva considerata
una transizione, un passaggio in attesa di una casa propria - la
coabitazione con altri - diventerebbe così, ridimensionate le
aspettative, un punto d'arrivo, una soluzione per la vita. A
dirlo è la ricerca sugli Under 40 in affitto "Collaborare e
abitare. Il diritto alla casa nelle metropoli per le nuove
generazioni", realizzata da Fondazione G. Feltrinelli con il
supporto di Fondazione Cariplo e a cura di Silvia Cafora del
Politecnico di Torino. Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: Dear rentals, young people think to cohabit
en_content:
In front of dear life and prices
Highest of the Immobiliare the young, however the under 40,
the solution of the
cohabitation 'fluid'. What was once considered
a transition, a passage waiting for a home - the
cohabitation with others - would become so, resize the
expectations, a point of arrival, a solution for life. A
it is the search for Under 40 for rent "Collaborate and
inhabit. The right to house in the metropolises for new
generations", realized by Fondazione G. Feltrinelli with

title: Caro affitti, i giovani ormai pensano al coabitare 'fluido' (2)
content:
"La ricerca - si legge in una nota 
delle due fondazioni - ha coinvolto per la prima volta in
tutt'Italia circa circa 500 giovani in affitto e di età compresa
tra i 23 e i 40
anni. Il rapporto, in primis, evidenzia come il trend di
mobilità giovanile in Italia sia crescente: aumenta la
maggioranza di soggetti che preferiscono vivere in affitto
anziché possedere la casa, soprattutto a causa della precarietà
economica e lavorativa che incide nella scelta di affitto per il
42%. Il curriculum abitativo di ognuno di noi si sta infatti
ampliando e dirigendo verso modelli di "abitare fluido": la
ricerca evidenzia come il 50% degli intervistati abbia cambiato
la città di residenza negli ultimi dieci anni, il 70% almeno una
volta nella vita. Il 41%, dei giovani, oggi, guarda con
interesse modalità abitative collaborative; il 32% valuterebbe
la possibilità di vivere in una casa in affitto con un progetto
collaborativo mentre il 38% in una casa di proprietà con un
progetto collaborativo". Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: Dear rentals, young people now think of 'fluid' cohabitation (2)
en_content:
"Research - read in a note
of the two foundations - it involved for the first time in
all Italy about 500 young people for rent and age
between 23 and 40
Years. The report, first, highlights how the trend of
youth mobility in Italy is growing: increased
majority of subjects who prefer to live in rent
instead of owning the house, especially because of precariousness
economic and working that affects the choice of rent for
42%. The living curriculum of each of us is in fact
extending and heading towards models of "fluid living": the
research highlights how 50% of respondents have changed
the city of residence in the last ten years, 70% at least one
time in life. 41% of young people, today, look with
interest in collaborative housing; 32% would rate
the possibility of living in a house for rent with a project
collaborative while 38% in a property house with a
collaborative project." Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA

regionale, con un'ottima spinta all'imprenditorialità, una
struttura demografica favorevole, con un migliore andamento in
valori assoluti del trend demografico rispetto alla media
dell'Umbria e una maggiore presenza di persone nelle fasce di
età cruciali per il lavoro, una migliore posizione nel ricambio
generazionale sia delle imprese che della forza lavoro, un
maggior dinamismo delle persone, una maggiore vitalità del
tessuto produttivo. Ma non mancano gli elementi di criticità:
sono alcuni degli elementi del report sull'economia
dell'Assisano (Assisi, Bastia Umbra, Cannara, Bettona),
presentato dall'Ente camerale dell'Umbria nella terza tappa - le
precedenti sono state a Città di Castello e Orvieto - svolta ad
Assisi nell'ambito dell'iniziativa "La Camera incontra i
territori". Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA

crucial age for work, a better position in the spare

title: Cosa sono gli ELTIF?
content: L’obiettivo di rendimento, reddito regolare e diversificazione da fonti alternative rispetto ai mercati tradizionali ha portato alla ribalta gli asset privati accelerando il processo di cambiamento che inizialmente vedeva soltanto i clienti istituzionali poter aver accesso a tale mercato. Ora invece alcuni tipi di Private Asset si stanno aprendo anche agli investitori retail. Nuove soluzioni come gli  Gli ELTIF così come spiegano gli esperti di  “Negli ultimi anni, è diventato sempre più chiaro ai governi il ruolo crescente dei capitali privati per contribuire alla crescita economica e alla creazione di posti di lavoro. Parallelamente a ciò, i gestori di fondi hanno lanciato strumenti pensati in modo più specifico per le esigenze degli investitori retail, più semplici in termini di accesso e di operatività. Ad esempio, con un unico conferimento di capitale e con importi minimi di sottoscrizione più bassi” spiegano gli esperti di Schroders.  Se entriamo nel dettaglio degli  E per gli investitori quali sono i vantaggi?  Secondo gli esperti della casa di gestione gli ELTIF rappresentano un progetto importante che contribuisce nel suo complesso a quella che viene definita la “democratizzazione dei private asset”, che porterà la linea di demarcazione tra mercati pubblici e privati ad essere sempre meno netta.
en_title: What are the ELTIFs?
en_content: The goal of return, regular income and diversification from alternative sources compared to traditional markets has led to the reversal of private assets by accelerating the process of change that initially saw only institutional customers being able to have access to that market. Some types of Private Assets are now also opening up to retail investors. New solutions such as the ELTIFs as well as explain the experts of “In recent years, it has become increasingly clear to governments the growing role of private capital to contribute to economic growth and job creation. In parallel to this, fund managers have launched tools designed more specifically for retail investors, easier in terms of access and operationality. For example, with a single bestowal of capital and with minimal amounts of lower subscription,” Schroders’ experts explain. If we go into the E detail for investors what are the benefits? According to management experts, the ELTIFs are an important project that contributes as a whole to what is called the “democratization of private assets”, which will lead the demarcation line between public and private markets to be less and less net.

title: L’investimento in biodiversità, un potenziale da sfruttare
content:
L’attività umana è la causa principale della perdita di    Ad esempio negli USA Gli acquirenti sono, in genere, trust fondiari o agenzie
ambientali statali o locali statunitensi. Gli acquisti possono
essere finanziati pubblicamente da un’agenzia governativa o
effettuati privatamente da una persona fisica o giuridica. Negli
ultimi anni, i programmi federali di finanziamento destinati alla
conservazione negli Stati Uniti hanno beneficiato di un sostegno
decisamente maggiore, grazie all’approvazione del Great American
Outdoors Act (2020) e dell’Infrastructure and Investment Jobs Act
(2021). Ma non è l’unica modalità. Esistono anche altri strumenti come i “I quadri di riferimento dei crediti di anidride carbonica per
la biodiversità costituiscono un altro approccio basato sul mercato
per incentivare gli investimenti nella natura. Gli incentivi
vengono creati versando crediti a progetti che generano risultati
positivi misurabili e scientificamente verificati a favore della
biodiversità. L’interesse per questo approccio è aumentato negli
ultimi anni, in parte a causa della crescita del mercato volontario
di anidride carbonica, ed è rafforzato dall’impellente necessità di
finanziare soluzioni che facciano progredire gli obiettivi politici
a livello di GBP volti ad arrestare e invertire la perdita di
biodiversità” spiega l’esperta di Nuveen Natural Capital e aggiunge
“  Secondo l’head of research and strategy di Nuveen Natural
Capital "Lo sviluppo di quadri di riferimento per i mercati dei
crediti a favore della biodiversità potrebbe aprire nuove strade
per investire nel ripristino ecologico, visto che quando tali
benefici aggiuntivi per la biodiversità possono essere verificati e
monetizzati sui mercati ambientali, gli investitori sono in grado
di generare un impatto positivo e conseguire un ricavo a livello
finanziario”. Per gli investitori in beni fondiari che cercano di destinare il
capitale a strategie a beneficio della biodiversità, esistono
numerose opportunità di accesso a mercati ambientali consolidati,
in particolare nei Paesi dei mercati sviluppati, come gli USA e
l’Australia. Ma nel breve termine è possibile osservare benefici
per la biodiversità legati ai crediti di CO2 in alcuni mercati
emergenti, come Brasile, Colombia e Indonesia, tra gli altri. I mercati ambientali per la protezione, la migliore gestione e
il ripristino della natura rappresentano un’opportunità sempre
maggiore per gli investitori nei beni fondiari. Investire nella
biodiversità può potenzialmente incrementare i ricavi delle
strategie d’investimento tradizionali — ed è dimostrato
empiricamente che può anche ridurre il rischio incrementando
l’esposizione verso mercati non correlati.
